ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED AND ADOPTED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED AS TO 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 6TH, AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION. CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. MOTION DENIED AS TO THE 1ST AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE NOT TORT-BASED BUT INSTEAD ARE CONTRACT-BASED CLAIMS. MOTION DENIED AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CIVIL CONSPIRACY WHICH IS PROPER UNDER CC SECTION 1714.10. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN 10 DAYS. MR. MINTZ TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KATY ANNE BERSAMIN, CSR #11722

Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PETER FLICKINGER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Appearances:
Walter Walker of Walker Hamilton and Koenig (986.3339) for plaintiff and responding party
Kevin R. Mintz of Rankin Sproat Mires Beaty & Reynolds (510.465.3922) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court modified and adopted its ruling as follows: Motion granted as to 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 6th, and 7th causes of action. Causes of action are barred by the litigation privilege. Motion denied as to the 1st and 8th causes of action that are not tort-based but instead are contract-based claims. Motion denied as to the 5th cause of action for civil conspiracy which is proper under CC section 1714.10. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend within 10 days. Mr. Mintz to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $790
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF DAVID ANDERSON Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $790 is GRANTED. (1) Defendant Flickinger is ordered to serve responses to Set Number One of Form Interrogatories within 10 days. (2) Plaintiff's requests for admissions to Defendant Flickinger are hereby deemed admitted. (3) Defendant Fickinger is ordered to pay sanctions of $500. Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICHAEL REISER'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Walter Herbert Walker, Esq. of Walker, Hamilton & White, (415) 986-3339; appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Michael Reiser, Esq., Defendant, appearing in Pro Per, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer to second, third, fourth, and sixth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff to plead with greater specificity facts showing causes of action not subject to litigation privilege (CC section 47). No court order required to allege conspiracy per exception: CC section 1714.10(c). Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Off Calendar Oct-27-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Walter Walker of Walker Hamilton & White (986.3339) for plaintiff and responding party
Michael J. Reiser (925.256.0400) defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MICHAEL REISER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained as to 2nd through 6th causes of action as to Reiser only with leave to amend to allege wrongdoing by Reiser outside of the litigation privilege, if plaintiff is able to do so. Ten days leave to amend. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/HEK/JW) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2008 Continued To Oct-27-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Walter Walker of Walker Hamilton & White (986.3339) for plaintiff and responding party
Blair Adams (773.1133) defendant in propria persona

This matter was not argued as notice of intent to argue was given late. At Mr. Walker's request for argument, the Court will continue DEFENDANT MICHAEL REISER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT to a date agreed upon by counsel. Mr. Walker to call department 302 with the new date by the end of the day. (302/HEK/JW) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

*** After hearing Mr. Walker notified the Court that the parties had agreed up on Monday, October 27, 2008 for the new hearing date ***
